Source Guide

Story & Source NoticeAbout Kyunolab

Mythic & Imagined Realms

Myths

Myths, creation stories, sacred narratives, heroes, gods, and symbolic folklore explained through meaning, versions, and lasting cultural patterns.

Myths is a Kyunolab Mystery Archive reading path for creation stories, sacred narratives, natural origins, culture heroes, gods, and symbolic explanations of the world. The collection approaches each subject through origin, meaning, common versions, cultural setting, and source status. It preserves the atmosphere that makes a legend memorable while separating documented context from oral tradition, community retelling, symbolic interpretation, and original narrative framing.

Readers can use this page to compare how similar motifs change across regions and formats. Some articles follow older folklore; others examine modern rumors, internet circulation, archival gaps, or stories attached to familiar places and objects. Titles and summaries are written to answer a clear question, but no repeated claim is treated as proof simply because it appears in many versions.

The entries below are ordered by their latest update. Each article links to related stories, narrower tags, source notes, and the wider archive shelf, making this category a starting point rather than a dead-end list. Begin with the topic closest to your question, then follow the connected motifs to see what changes, what persists, and where the available evidence stops.

Because the archive includes legends, retellings, symbolic readings, and evidence-limited mysteries, source labels remain visible throughout. That distinction lets readers enjoy the story pattern without confusing cultural importance, online popularity, or local tradition with independent verification.

Myths

Demeter and Persephone: Abduction, Seasons, and the Myth of Return With a Condition

Demeter and Persephone is a pre-existing Myths subject built around the Greek myth in which Persephone taken to the underworld and Demeter grief becomes tied to seasonal change and return.

Myths - Greek Seasonal Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 21, 2026
Myths

Maui Slows the Sun: Rope, Dawn, and the Myth That Made the Day Longer

Maui Slows the Sun is a pre-existing Myths subject built around the Polynesian myth in which Maui restrains or slows the sun so people have enough daylight for work and life.

Myths - Polynesian Culture Hero Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 21, 2026
Myths

Osiris and Isis: Dismemberment, Search, and the Egyptian Myth of Restoration

Osiris and Isis is a pre-existing Myths subject built around the Egyptian myth cycle in which Isis searches for Osiris after his murder and restoration becomes part of kingship and afterlife meaning.

Myths - Egyptian Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 21, 2026
Myths

King Midas and the Golden Touch: Wish, Hunger, and the Myth of Desire Turning Heavy

King Midas is a pre-existing Myths subject built around the Greek myth of a king whose wish turns everything he touches into gold until the gift becomes a curse.

Myths - Greek Moral Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 21, 2026
Myths

Heracles and the Twelve Labors: Impossible Tasks, Atonement, and the Hero Tested Again and Again

Heracles and the Twelve Labors is a pre-existing Myths subject built around the Greek hero cycle in which Heracles completes a series of impossible labors as punishment, proof, and mythic trial.

Myths - Greek Hero Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 21, 2026
Myths

Sisyphus Myth: The Stone, the Hill, and the Punishment That Never Ends

Sisyphus is a Greek mythic figure condemned in the underworld to roll a stone uphill, only for it to fall back again.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 20, 2026
Myths

Persephone and the Underworld: The Greek Myth Behind Loss, Return, and the Seasons

The Persephone myth tells how Persephone is taken to the underworld, how Demeter grieves, and how Persephone returns for part of the year. The cycle is often connected with seasonal change.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Icarus Flight Myth: Wax Wings, the Sun, and the Warning About Flying Too High

Icarus is the Greek mythic figure who escapes Crete with wings made by Daedalus, but flies too close to the sun. The wax melts, the wings fail, and Icarus falls into the sea.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Phaethon Sun Chariot Myth: Fire, Fall, and the Danger of Driving the Sun

Phaethon is a Greek mythic figure who asks to drive the chariot of the sun and loses control. The burning path threatens the world until Zeus strikes him down.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Prometheus Fire Myth: Theft, Punishment, and the Gift That Changed Humanity

Prometheus is a Greek mythic figure remembered for stealing fire and giving it to humanity. Zeus punishes him for crossing divine limits, often by binding him while an eagle tears at him.

Myths - Greek Myth - 8 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Narcissus and Echo: Reflection, Voice, and the Greek Myth of Unreachable Love

Narcissus and Echo is a Greek myth about two forms of unreachable love. Echo can only repeat the words of others, while Narcissus becomes trapped by his own reflection and cannot reach the image he desires.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Medusa and Perseus: The Gorgon, the Mirrored Shield, and the Power of the Severed Head

A source-aware Kyunolab article tracing Medusa, Perseus, the Gorgon sisters, the mirrored shield, and the strange afterlife of the severed head.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
